reachability_fact: STDLIB/iii/aether/pattern_set_federation.iii:114-124 — `pattern_set_fed_publish` allocates a slot and writes PSF_LIVE[s]=1u8 with no call to `fed_seal_lookup_anchor`. The extern `fed_seal_lookup_anchor` IS declared at L33 (the same call pattern_set_fed_fetch uses at L222), so the gate is available but intentionally absent in publish. Corpus 247:68 and 247:91 are live callers that call publish without pre-anchoring, confirming the ungated path is reachable in the test suite. Corpus 946:22 also publishes an unvouched set expecting success (h != 0 at line 23), proving the slot IS allocated for unvouched input. FETCH enforces the gate (L222-223); PUBLISH does not (L114-124). The invariant "PSF_LIVE[s] == 1u8 implies a fed_seal anchor exists for (PSF_TIER[s], mhash)" is broken by every publish call.
maximal_design: Gate pattern_set_fed_publish with the same fed_seal_lookup_anchor call that pattern_set_fed_fetch enforces. This makes the registry invariant unconditional: every slot is live iff it has a FOUNDERS-ANCHOR-descended seal entry. The lifecycle contract is: callers MUST call fed_seal_anchor(child_tier, mhash_ptr, parent_tier, parent_root_ptr) before pattern_set_fed_publish; this is already possible because fed_seal_anchor is an exported function. No ABI change to pattern_set_fed_publish's signature — callers that pre-anchor already work; callers that do not pre-anchor get 0 back (the same fail sentinel as all other error paths). The existing extern fed_seal_lookup_anchor at L33 is reused verbatim — no new extern needed. pattern_set_fed_verify_ancestry becomes a redundancy check (the invariant is now enforced at admission time) rather than the only post-hoc gate. Corpus 946 is reworked to prove BOTH the negative arm (unvouched publish returns 0, no slot allocated) and the positive arm (pre-anchored publish succeeds, verify_ancestry confirms). Corpus 247 is updated to pre-anchor the mhash before publishing; the test structure is preserved but adds fed_seal_clear + fed_seal_anchor calls before step 1b. This subsystem is strictly better: the invariant holds for both inbound (fetch from peer) and outbound (local publish) registration paths, with no bootstrap hole.

code_changes.anchor: /* PUBLISH: register a locally-held pattern set into the federation
/* 946 -- W4.2 / ENHANCE-17 falsifier: aether/pattern_set_federation's FETCH no longer omits

kat:
Corpus 946 (946_pattern_set_fed_ancestry.iii) — extended to prove both arms:

NEGATIVE (the fix fires on bad input):
  1. fed_seal_clear() + pattern_set_fed_reset(): registry empty, count == 0.
  2. pattern_set_fed_publish(0u64, 1u32, &PSF_M) where PSF_M is a fresh 32-byte mhash with NO fed_seal anchor: MUST return 0 (rejected). count stays 0.
  3. pattern_set_fed_verify_ancestry(0u64) == 0 (null handle rejected).
  4. pattern_set_fed_verify_ancestry(9999u64) == 0 (out-of-range handle rejected).

POSITIVE (the anchored path is admitted):
  5. fed_seal_anchor(1u32, &PSF_M, 2u32, &PSF_PR) == 0 (anchor established).
  6. pattern_set_fed_publish(0u64, 1u32, &PSF_M) returns non-zero handle h.
  7. pattern_set_fed_count() == 1.
  8. pattern_set_fed_verify_ancestry(h) == 1 (live slot is confirmed anchored).
  Exit 99 = all pass.

Corpus 247 (247_first_domain_pattern_set.iii) — updated lifecycle:
  Before step 1b publish: fed_seal_anchor(1u32, &C247_MHASH, 2u32, &C247_PARENT) == 0.
  All subsequent publish/discover/mhash-readback/tier/verify_ancestry/duplicate-publish/reset assertions follow; pattern_set_fed_verify_ancestry(disc) == 1 added as step 2d.
  Exit 99 = all pass.
